title: Beautiful
text: Beautiful place despite a fairly complicated sandy road. We were advised the long road to get there, wider but just as difficult.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

original_text: Beautiful place despite a fairly complicated sandy road. We were advised the long road to get there, wider but just as difficult.
translated_text: Beautiful place despite a fairly complicated sandy road. We were advised the long road to get there, wider but just as difficult.

title: Frustrating booking experience and unhelpful company
text: We booked one of the Baine's Baobab campsites via their forms online and got a confirmation via email. When we reached the gate after a long drive, all the Baobab campsites were booked and we were told we could only stay at South camp which was too far at the time and less well rated. The person responsible hardly apologised and didn't offer any solution for something that's clearly an error on their side (other than staying at South camp for the regular price). Apparently an "error" that happens frequently. Frustrating experience.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: A special place to visit once in your lifetime
text: Driving to Baines Baobabs can be a bit challenging due to the road conditions, but once you get there you have the feeling you are the only one on this planet. No wildlife, just great views!

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

original_title: A special place to visit once in your lifetime

translated_title: A special place to visit once in your lifetime
translated_text: Driving to Baines Baobabs can be a bit challenging due to the road conditions, but once you get there you have the feeling you are the only one on this planet. No wildlife, just great views!

title: Standing proud on the edge of a salt pan
text:
After leaving Makgadikadi on a heavily sandy road, 20km on the A3 and we entered Nxai Pan Park. A daily entrance fee covers both parks on the same day. We didn't have overnight accommodation, all full, so would visit for the day. But there were stern warnings that Nxai Pan was tough, very sandy, 4 hours vehicle and trailer stuck the previous day so we aimed for just Baines Boabab.
The drive was OK, not too difficult. Although one stupid French man who had been driving in a ridiculous manner earlier in the day was now firmly stuck. Despite my inexperience, now 5 days 4x4 driving, I pulled him out. The boababs were excellent, standing proud on the edge of a salt pan they were huge, reputed to be over 1000 years old, excellent.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Great views terrible roads
text: Baines Baobab the scenery is excellent and the toilets although long drops were good. The roads in are rutted and really filled with sand. I don’t think a grader has been there for months. If you driving a 4by 2 it’s going to be a struggle. Try entering early before sand gets hot.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Definitely would not recommend this overpriced campsite full of flies
text:
We definitely would not recommend staying at this campsite. Upon arrival, we were swarmed by hundreds of flies and other stinging insects, making it impossible to enjoy the site. We ended up staying in the car to avoid them and decided it was best to leave. When we mentioned this at the entrance, there was no understanding of our situation, and we were told to find alternative accommodation, despite having paid for two days of park entry and one night of camping.
Additionally, the booking process was frustrating; I initially had contact with Godairone, who suddenly stopped responding. After a few weeks, my boyfriend contacted her and surprisingly we immediately got a reply. Overall, we really recommend not staying on this overpriced campsite.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Unmissable in Nxai Pan
text:
A night at campsite no. 1: alone in the world!
Accessible only by 4x4, the road allows us to already get in the mood. 
Once you arrive, it’s magical, huge baobab trees dominate the Pan.
Campsite number 1 is magnificent and very well equipped: pitch - bush toilet - bush shower. A very beautiful baobab just for this location!
An extraordinary experience and an exceptional view of the Milky Way.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: If you have things to do in the area
text:
On the way to Nxay pan campsite, a visit to Baines Baobab is essential. But we’re not just going there for that.
The road is not easy and requires a very good vehicle with good ground clearance.
On site, these baobabs are impressive. The dry sides are too.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Super experience but 2 nights min (pref - 3). Must be fully self-sufficient. Tough drives, but so worth it
text:
Drove here from Kubbu Island (not easy or straight forward), via Gweta (unremarkable), the A3 (dead straight & better Road surface than expected) and Nxai Pan National Park Gates - nice warm Welcome and pleasant Chap signed us in, took our Fees/monies, & directed us - Left was longer and less sand - Right was shorter but quite deep sand (Our 4x4 Land Cruiser was deemed sufficient for either option). NB. Tyre pressures.

Shorter Option was tougher but only 12 Kms, so we took it. Camp site - # 1 is Booked so we claim #2.

3 Hrs later we declare #2 a Mystery - we did not get stuck but progress was unbelievably slow. We did find #1 and with no one else in sight, we claimed it (no one ever showed-up). We never did find #2. Word of Warning we thought it incredible easy to get lost around Nxai and Kubbu - whole of Makgadikgadi Pans area.

Site #2 was stunning location, great sunset, dawn, & sunrise. Ablutions fine (for long-drop). We were a self-drive, self-catered, self-guided, & self-sufficient Team. For this Location, one needs to be. Just you the space, sky, stars, & silence. - surreal.

Loved it, but we made the mistake of only booking 1 x night. Too rushed. Commend 3 x nights minimum, then you have the time to visit Nxai Pan proper & the Watering Holes up there (to the North), where there was quite a bit of Game. we saw very little. Note that extra Drive adds about another 2/3 Hrs.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Einsamkeit pur
text:
Wunderschöne Gegend,Salzpfanne beeindruckend,Bäume gigantisch.
Campingplätze sollten besser ausgeschildert werden.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

Wunderschöne Gegend,Salzpfanne beeindruckend,Bäume gigantisch.

translated_text: Beautiful area, impressive salt pan, gigantic trees. Campsites should be better signposted.

title: Day trip
text: Truly one of the most beautiful places in Botswana. Seeing the baobab trees growing in the middle of nowhere was just exceptional. We highly recommend this place for a day drip and chill.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

original_text: Truly one of the most beautiful places in Botswana. Seeing the baobab trees growing in the middle of nowhere was just exceptional. We highly recommend this place for a day drip and chill.
translated_text: Truly one of the most beautiful places in Botswana. Seeing the baobab trees growing in the middle of nowhere was just exceptional. We highly recommend this place for a day drip and chill.

title: Zalige plek
text:
Een zalige plek om een nacht te verblijven. 
De campsites bevinden zich op begroeide eilandjes met een of meerdere baobabs midden van een grote zoutpanne.
Geen probleem want de panne is perfect berijdbaar. Wel oppassen voor stenen.
Op de campsite sta je helemaal alleen onder een enorme baobab.
De voorzieningen zijn zeer basic en je moet alles meebrengen (water, eten, brandhout). De nachtelijke hemel is schitterend!
Geniet zeker ook van de zonsopkomst.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

original_text:
Een zalige plek om een nacht te verblijven. 
De campsites bevinden zich op begroeide eilandjes met een of meerdere baobabs midden van een grote zoutpanne.
Geen probleem want de panne is perfect berijdbaar. Wel oppassen voor stenen.
Op de campsite sta je helemaal alleen onder een enorme baobab.
De voorzieningen zijn zeer basic en je moet alles meebrengen (water, eten, brandhout). De nachtelijke hemel is schitterend!
Geniet zeker ook van de zonsopkomst.
translated_text: A blissful place to spend a night. The campsites are located on overgrown islets with one or more baobabs in the middle of a large salt pan. No problem, as the pan is perfectly drivable. Do watch out for stones, though. At the campsite, you are completely alone under an enormous baobab. The facilities are very basic, and you have to bring everything with you (water, food, firewood). The night sky is magnificent! Be sure to enjoy the sunrise as well.

title: An otherwordly oasis
text: Cool spot. Had a nice late lunch with my guide and spotter from the Kwando camp within the NP. It is quite far away though-so basically an all day drive.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

original_text: Cool spot. Had a nice late lunch with my guide and spotter from the Kwando camp within the NP. It is quite far away though-so basically an all day drive.
translated_text: Cool spot. Had a nice late lunch with my guide and spotter from the Kwando camp within the NP. It is quite far away though-so basically an all day drive.

title: Nxai Pan with African Bush Camps
text:
I recently spent 4 days in Nxai Pan with African Bush Camps, and didn’t want to leave. The camps are mobile: they change every week or so, and the pan, (my first salt pan experience!) was lovely. Lots of open views, prides of lions, springbok, raptors, bee eaters, bat-eared foxes and jackals. Very few other people.  ABC took great care of me: You gotta love the combination of canvas walls and compost toilets with a wine steward and remarkably tasty meals. 
My guide, Ken, was expert at spotting all manner of wildlife, wonderful company, and intuitively knew how best to position the vehicle for optimal photo opps.  I am very grateful for his good work!  I was a little early for the zebra migration:  they were arriving just as I had to move on, but this is a special place, well worth revisiting.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: un lieu magique à visiter
text:
Un endroit incroyable perdu au milieu de nulle part. Nous y avons passé un moement magique. Mais attention, le lieu est difficile d'accès : il faut avoir un très bon 4x4 et maitriser la conduite sur routes sablonneuse avec parfois du sable profond.
Ne surtout pas s'aventurer sur les pans aux alentours des baobabs en dehors des traces.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

original_text:
Un endroit incroyable perdu au milieu de nulle part. Nous y avons passé un moement magique. Mais attention, le lieu est difficile d'accès : il faut avoir un très bon 4x4 et maitriser la conduite sur routes sablonneuse avec parfois du sable profond.
Ne surtout pas s'aventurer sur les pans aux alentours des baobabs en dehors des traces.
translated_text: An incredible place lost in the middle of nowhere. We had a magical time there. But be warned, the place is difficult to access: you need a very good 4x4 and be skilled at driving on sandy roads, sometimes with deep sand. Do not venture onto the slopes around the baobabs except on the marked tracks.
